mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in >65% of monthly observations over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by >0.5% in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward returns across 80% of monthly observations in a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, persistent inflation pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 shows positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances where INDPRO drops by more than 0.5% month-over-month.
